title: Miner Decentralization
description: Miner decentralization means transaction ordering is performed by a changing, open set of miners rather than one coordinator. It limits censorship power, while independently validating nodes still determine whether proposed blocks obey consensus rules.

resources.title: Bitcoin Development Philosophy: Miner Decentralization
resources.notes: Explains why a dynamic, permissionless mining set protects transaction ordering from centralized censorship.

resources.title: Bitcoin Whitepaper
resources.notes: Defines proof-of-work's distributed timestamping and longest-chain model.

resources.title: Stratum V2 Specification
resources.notes: Implementation context for mining-pool protocols and job negotiation; not required to understand the broader concept.

resources.title: Bitcoin Development Philosophy
resources.notes: Long-form treatment of decentralization, censorship resistance, and the distinct roles of miners and full nodes.
